an advisory refinement. Across multiple Fizeau processes, correct stickiness and
fair balancing require a shared lease backend; raw server metrics alone are
sampled and racy.

**D13: Protocol capabilities are type-keyed and conservative.** The provider
exposes `SupportsTools()`, `SupportsStream()`, and
`SupportsStructuredOutput()` accessors that return the effective capability for
the resolved type. Downstream routing consults these before dispatch to avoid
dispatch-and-fail on mismatched prompts. Unknown types return `false` for all
protocol flags so routing rejects rather than dispatches. This surface is
distinct from benchmark-based capability scoring.

**RequiresTools filter scope.** `RequiresTools=true` filters candidates at the
`(harness, provider source, endpoint, model)` level via an OR-permissive gate:
a candidate passes when either `routing.HarnessEntry.SupportsTools` or
`routing.ProviderEntry.SupportsTools` is `true`, and the catalog's per-model
override is not set.

**D14: `DetectedType()` layers on top of `providerSystem` without replacing
it.** `providerSystem` remains the source of truth for per-response telemetry
and cost attribution because those fire on every response and cannot afford a
network probe. `DetectedType()` is the probe-confirmed accessor used for
pre-dispatch gating. It runs the probe at most once per provider via
`sync.Once`, caches the result, and falls back to `providerSystem` when the
probe is inconclusive.

**D15: `reasoning` is the public model-reasoning control.** The public surface
uses one scalar (`reasoning`) for named and numeric values. Config uses
`reasoning`; catalog metadata uses `reasoning_default`; the CLI uses
`--reasoning`. Provider and harness adapters may translate the resolved value
to wire or subprocess knobs named `thinking`, `effort`, `variant`, or numeric
budgets, but those names are not preferred public controls. Unsupported
auto/default controls may be dropped; explicit unsupported or over-limit
values fail clearly.

data.text: I have enough context to patch this cleanly. The edit is limited to three pieces: add a local prompt-token heuristic plus explicit tool-requirement derivation in `agentcli/run.go`, clear stale pre-service selection fields when the service fails before routing, and add focused CLI tests around those seams.

I’m about to patch `agentcli/run.go` and the run-path tests. The changes are confined to the request builder and the pre-dispatch failure cleanup; I’m not touching service routing logic or DDx forwarding.

After installing Fizeau v0.14.3, the live smoke `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` no longer invokes Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`, but it still fails before dispatch with `ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at policy default`. The prompt text is nonempty, so Fizeau's own CLI run path is still sending an unset prompt feature envelope into route selection.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`agentcli/run.go:408-441` constructs `serviceExecuteRequestParams` from the parsed prompt, policy, and harness, but it does not compute or pass prompt feature fields. `agentcli/run.go:590-620` defines `serviceExecuteRequestParams` without `EstimatedPromptTokens` or `RequiresTools`, and `agentcli/run.go:675-690` builds `fizeau.ServiceExecuteRequest` without setting those fields. `service.go:817-823` documents that `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` drive auto-selection gates, and `service_routing.go:1761-1773` forwards those fields directly into `RouteRequest`; the current CLI path therefore forwards zero/false regardless of a nonempty prompt or tool preset.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the CLI service-run envelope populate prompt feature fields before calling `Execute`: compute a conservative nonzero `EstimatedPromptTokens` from `Prompt` + `SystemPrompt` when no stronger estimate is available, and set `RequiresTools` when explicit tools or a tool preset will be available to the run. Thread both fields through `serviceExecuteRequestParams` and `buildServiceExecuteRequest`. Keep route selection in Fizeau service/routing; this bead only fixes the CLI request envelope and failure metadata.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx request forwarding; DDx's analogous bug is already tracked separately. Do not pin provider/model/harness to bypass routing. Do not change policy scoring or provider health refresh behavior.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Add `TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures` proving `buildServiceExecuteRequest` copies `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` into `fizeau.ServiceExecuteRequest` while preserving the existing harness+policy empty-model behavior.
2. Add `TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest` proving `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` reaches the service execution stub with `Harness="codex"`, `Policy="default"`, `Model=""`, `Provider=""`, `EstimatedPromptTokens > 0`, and `RequiresTools == false`.
3. Add `TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest` proving a run with an enabled tool preset or explicit tool support sets `RequiresTools == true` in the service request.
4. If `Execute` returns a route-resolution error before dispatch, JSON output must not invent an unrelated `selected_provider`, `selected_route`, or `resolved_model` from a stale pre-service selection; add `TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection` or an equivalent focused regression.
5. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz -run 'TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures|TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection' -count=1` passes.
6. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` either succeeds or fails before dispatch, but its error/output must not contain `prompt of 0 tokens` and must not invoke or report `qwen3.5-27b`.
7. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz . -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:cli, area:routing, area:service, kind:bug, incident:zero-token-route, downstream:ddx</labels>     <metadata parent="fizeau-361f4f4b" spec-id="FEAT-004" base-rev="77cda3d3f1cc96e2434643e185576b083f595634" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T105653-1d111aac"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-004" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md">Feature Specification: FEAT-004 — Shared Model Catalog and Policy Routing</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

During live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.
2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.
3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare" -count=1` passes.
5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:git, area:execution, kind:bug, operator-ux, dirty-tree</labels>     <metadata base-rev="2e2468b9adc5de9db9553582f1aa4a902abadc3d" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T110023-a0db107f"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. ## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T105653-1d111aac/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T105653-1d111aac/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="fizeau-737b19b1">     <title>run: forward CLI prompt features into service routing</title>     <description> PROBLEM
After installing Fizeau v0.14.3, the live smoke `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` no longer invokes Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`, but it still fails before dispatch with `ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at policy default`. The prompt text is nonempty, so Fizeau's own CLI run path is still sending an unset prompt feature envelope into route selection.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`agentcli/run.go:408-441` constructs `serviceExecuteRequestParams` from the parsed prompt, policy, and harness, but it does not compute or pass prompt feature fields. `agentcli/run.go:590-620` defines `serviceExecuteRequestParams` without `EstimatedPromptTokens` or `RequiresTools`, and `agentcli/run.go:675-690` builds `fizeau.ServiceExecuteRequest` without setting those fields. `service.go:817-823` documents that `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` drive auto-selection gates, and `service_routing.go:1761-1773` forwards those fields directly into `RouteRequest`; the current CLI path therefore forwards zero/false regardless of a nonempty prompt or tool preset.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the CLI service-run envelope populate prompt feature fields before calling `Execute`: compute a conservative nonzero `EstimatedPromptTokens` from `Prompt` + `SystemPrompt` when no stronger estimate is available, and set `RequiresTools` when explicit tools or a tool preset will be available to the run. Thread both fields through `serviceExecuteRequestParams` and `buildServiceExecuteRequest`. Keep route selection in Fizeau service/routing; this bead only fixes the CLI request envelope and failure metadata.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx request forwarding; DDx's analogous bug is already tracked separately. Do not pin provider/model/harness to bypass routing. Do not change policy scoring or provider health refresh behavior.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Add `TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures` proving `buildServiceExecuteRequest` copies `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` into `fizeau.ServiceExecuteRequest` while preserving the existing harness+policy empty-model behavior.
2. Add `TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest` proving `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` reaches the service execution stub with `Harness="codex"`, `Policy="default"`, `Model=""`, `Provider=""`, `EstimatedPromptTokens > 0`, and `RequiresTools == false`.
3. Add `TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest` proving a run with an enabled tool preset or explicit tool support sets `RequiresTools == true` in the service request.
4. If `Execute` returns a route-resolution error before dispatch, JSON output must not invent an unrelated `selected_provider`, `selected_route`, or `resolved_model` from a stale pre-service selection; add `TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection` or an equivalent focused regression.
5. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz -run 'TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures|TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection' -count=1` passes.
6. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` either succeeds or fails before dispatch, but its error/output must not contain `prompt of 0 tokens` and must not invoke or report `qwen3.5-27b`.
7. `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz . -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:cli, area:routing, area:service, kind:bug, incident:zero-token-route, downstream:ddx</labels>     <metadata parent="fizeau-361f4f4b" spec-id="FEAT-004" base-rev="77cda3d3f1cc96e2434643e185576b083f595634" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T105653-1d111aac"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-004" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md">Feature Specification: FEAT-004 — Shared Model Catalog and Policy Routing</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

data.text:
Implemented the CLI envelope fix in [agentcli/run.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-737b19b1-20260515T105653-1d111aac/agentcli/run.go): it now computes a coarse prompt-token estimate from prompt + system prompt, forwards `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` into `ServiceExecuteRequest`, and avoids leaking stale pre-service routing metadata into service results. I added focused regressions in [agentcli/run_prompt_features_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-737b19b1-20260515T105653-1d111aac/agentcli/run_prompt_features_test.go) for prompt-feature forwarding, tool-requirement forwarding, and the pre-dispatch JSON failure case.

Verified with:
- `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz -run 'TestBuildServiceExecuteRequest_ForwardsPromptFeatures|TestRun_PassesPromptFeaturesIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_PassesToolRequirementIntoServiceRequest|TestRun_JSONResolveRouteFailureDoesNotReportStaleSelection|TestCLI_Run_ModelByModelName|TestCLI_BackendRoutingAttributionFlowsIntoResultAndSession|TestCLI_Providers_Check_ModelsUseConfiguredProviderWithoutRunningModelResolution' -count=1`
- `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz . -count=1`
- `go run ./cmd/fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p 'Reply with OK.' --max-iter 1 --json`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

The repo-binary smoke succeeded and returned `OK` on `gpt-5.4-mini`; it no longer reported `prompt of 0 tokens` or `qwen3.5-27b`. Commit: `038a0875`
